rationale: Both titles are virtually identical and strong: they name the intervention comparison (PFA vs RFA), the population (drug-resistant paroxysmal AF), the study type (multicentre randomized trial), and the acronym (BEAT PAROX-AF). Y adds the single word 'controlled,' making the study design descriptor 'randomized controlled trial' — the standard, internationally recognised CONSORT/ICMJE terminology. Europace, as a rigorous peer-reviewed journal, expects precise methodological language; 'randomized controlled trial' is the accepted designation and marginally raises precision and credibility over the slightly incomplete 'randomized trial.' All other dimensions are equal.
loser_weakness: X omits 'controlled' from the trial descriptor, leaving 'randomized trial' — technically acceptable but less precise than the universally standard 'randomized controlled trial.' This minor omission is the sole weakness distinguishing it from Y.
